feedback.text: Salut, this would be the case if the tax ruling regulates practices regarding the closure of online services for games (see the stop destroying game initiative). But also in other cases, citizens are deprived of their digital or PHYSIQUE property. With the advent of smart objects or who require to be connected to the internet to function (and thus to be connected to the brand server), consumers are increasingly likely to be stolen (or at least degraded). An example: — voice assistants – telephones that are no longer up to date and cannot be switched to another bone (recently Samsung) – (new) vacuum robots – connected household appliances (washing machine, oven) – connected mixers/cookers – new high-end cars – new tractors – US F-35:))) Even if it is not possible to have Avitam æternam servers, it is possible to allow the user to switch to at least the applications that run on the servers (not the code or industrial secrets, just the application). Given that they are 99 % on external nouds (AWS, azure, OVH) this should not be hard for companies even if they have to change some tips. Even if applications are complex or difficult to install, there will always be volunteers to take over the flame and run servers for others (e.g. the plethora of games that have unofficial servers). P.S.: It would therefore be necessary to oblige undertakings to let the user choose their services (as with the apple store) but for other purposes. For example, watches connected with premium subscription (Garmin, Fitbit) or your car with options already present that sleep 2000 to be activated...
